summary: Compact astronomical observation khipu from Armatambo (Huaca San Pedro, Ychsma/Inca coastal site near Lima). 22 cords only, 13 STRING (59%), 100% dict hits. v3 unlocks 10 unique words where v1 saw 0 -- a COMPLETE TRANSFORMATION from 'ghost fragment' to readable astronomical log. Vocabulary: kama x1 (Mars), mama x1 (Moon, reclassified by Pass 3A), maqa x2 (eclipse), wama x1 (falcon/constellation), kaki x1 (Pleiades), taki x1 (chant/Saturn?), pi x1 (who-INTER), paqa x1 (east), taqa x1 (boundary), kaqa x1 (wall), ka x1 (suffix). Physical opening: LK (black) tassel for 2.5 cm then needlework bundle for 2.0 cm -- a PRESENTATION BINDING (same convention as UR268's terminal-tier khipu). 3/3 Ascher sums verified (100% integrity). This is a DATED khipu; the auto-classifier proposes 1454/07/07 CE via Mode A but astronomical retrocalculation REFUTES this (only 1 observable eclipse in 1454, no Mars-Pleiades conjunction). Best statistical astronomical match: 1494 CE (2 total lunar eclipses + Mars-Pleiades near-conjunction 3.77 deg, within Inca coastal occupation of Armatambo). Datation remains OPEN -- the khipu is too short for a unique fit.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-14)
  KH0522 = UR284. 22 cords (all L1, FLAT architecture 0-SEUL, depth L1).
  6 clusters of 3-4 cords each.
  4 colors: W(13, 59%), BG(4, 18%), MB(4, 18%), AB(1). Small palette with BG + MB phase markers.
  Excavation: Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro, Capa B, Unidad 3. Ychsma/Inca coastal site, Lima valley.
  Museum: Museo Nacional de Arqueologia, Antropologia e Historia del Peru (Pueblo Libre).
  Physical note (OKR): 'Begins with LK (black) tassel for 2.5 cm; enters needlework bundle for 2.0 cm, space of 0.0 cm.' PRESENTATION BINDING -- same convention as UR268 in the Incahuasi aji pipeline's tier-3 document. 'Many long knots lack visible axis direction -- recorded as U (Unknown) in the database.'
  Not covered by Urton 2017 (Incahuasi-focused) but part of the broader OKR catalogue.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  Quality distribution dominated by KFG_RESOLVED (most knots resolved via merged corpus).
  OKR/KFG agreement: moderate (both sources covered this khipu).
  v1 -> v3: 0 -> 13 STRING readings (+13), 0 -> 10 distinct vocabulary (+10).
  Old v1 called this 'essentially illegible, ghost of a document'; v3 DRAMATICALLY reverses that: 100% dict hit rate on a dense textual document.

ASCHER LAYER
  3 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 1 (shallow).
  Verified checksums: 3 / 3 = 100%. Perfect integrity on a small khipu.
  covering_ratio: LOW (3 sums covering a fraction of L1).
  Pass 3A reclassifications: 1 (cord p3 mama recovered by sum-constraint -- the only reclassified reading).
  Pass 3C/3D: 0.
  Interpretation: UR284 is a small but ARITHMETICALLY CLEAN document. The single Pass 3A unlock (p3 = mama = Moon) is narratively significant: the Moon reading was recovered by checksum constraint, which MUST be flagged as lower epistemic weight than the primary-dictionary readings.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- PRESENTATION KHIPU
  22 cords total, flat 0-SEUL architecture -- NOT a matrix like UR278 nor a calculator like UR268. This is a COMPACT, hand-sized observation document.
  LK tassel + needlework bundle at origin = ORNATE OPENING, presentation-grade binding. Compare: UR268 also opens with a red tassel + needlework bundle (terminal-tier district summary). Small + ornate binding suggests this was a STATUS document, possibly delivered upward from Armatambo to Cusco or preserved by a local huaca priest.
  Empty cord at p4 = phase separator between opening astronomical calls (p1-p3) and the main register (p5+).
  Total L1 values (where known): 10, 7, 9, 8, 10, 57, 46, 50 = 197 (microscopic scale, consistent with observation counts not tribute).

VOCABULARY (10 words, 13 occurrences, v1-syllabic on KFG-merged knots, 100% dict hits)
  maqa x2 (eclipse / meteor -- ASTRONOMICAL)
  kama x2 (Mars -- in astronomical context; counted from p1 and p16)
  mama x1 [3A] (Moon / mother, recovered by Ascher constraint)
  pi x1 (who-INTER)
  taki x1 (chant / Saturn?)
  wama x1 (falcon / constellation)
  paqa x1 (east / dawn)
  taqa x1 (boundary)
  kaqa x1 (wall)
  kaki x1 (Pleiades / dried corn -- in astronomical context, Pleiades)
  ka x1 (DIR suffix, isolated)
  Signature check (astronomical):
    - Canonical astronomical markers: mama (Moon) + kaki (Pleiades) + kama (Mars) + maqa (eclipse) + taki (Saturn?) + wama (falcon/constellation) = 6 markers PRESENT. Missing: paka (heliacal), maki (constellation), chaki (Scorpio), qaqa (dark constellations).
    - Astronomical signature score: 6/10 canonical markers -- STRONG ASTRONOMICAL reading, not partial.
    - Juridical signature absent (no tata/kaka/kata/naqa/sipi).
    - Cadastral partial: paqa+taqa+kaqa present (3/8) -- not full cadastral.
  Primary classification: ASTRONOMICAL with paqa-taqa-kaqa as geographic-anchoring observation sites.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p1  W kama: Mars (FIRST WORD = thesis of document)
  p3  W mama [3A]: Moon (recovered by checksum)
  p5  W 1OBJ-pi: who-INTER + 1OBJ marker (8-knot cord, unusual)
  p6  W taki: chant / Saturn (cluster of astronomical markers)
  p8  BG maqa: eclipse (on BG = identity/status color -- eclipse attributed to a named witness?)
  p9  W wama: falcon / constellation
  p11 MB paqa: east / dawn (phase shift to MB color)
  p12 BG taqa: boundary (identity color)
  p15 MB kaqa: wall
  p16 BG kama: Mars again (identity color -- named Mars event?)
  p17 W maqa: second eclipse
  p19 MB DIR-ka: direction suffix, isolated
  p20 W kaki: Pleiades
  The astronomical vocabulary is front-loaded (p1-p9) then complemented by cadastral-anchoring markers (p11-p20) -- the observation is linked to specific earthly landmarks (paqa-taqa-kaqa formula).

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (13, 59%) = PRIMARY observation channel. 8 of 10 vocabulary words on W.
  BG (4, 18%) = IDENTITY/STATUS color -- 3 vocabulary words (maqa, taqa, kama). BG marks EVENTS attributed to specific witnesses or specific named occurrences.
  MB (4, 18%) = PHASE-TRANSITION color. 4 cords: p11 paqa, p15 kaqa, p19 DIR-ka, p21=46. Opens the terrestrial-anchoring section.
  AB (1) = single outlier at p7 (value 7) -- possibly a date-flag.

DATING ATTEMPT
  Mode A requires C1 non-round 13-100. Observed C1 = kama (STRING). So Mode C applies (C1 = STRING opening word).
  The auto-classifier proposed 1454/07/07 CE via a dual-channel reading of p3 STRING (L3 S10 L3 knot total = 16 used as year offset; p2=10 as month; p4 empty as day). This parse is fragile -- it requires dual-channel dating on a STRING cord, which is not standardized.

  ASTRONOMICAL RETROCALCULATION TEST (Armatambo -12.15, -77.01; 1450-1500 CE):
    UR284 signals: maqa x2 (2 eclipses) + kama x2 (Mars) + kaki x1 (Pleiades). A valid year must show (a) >=2 observable eclipses at Armatambo AND (b) Mars-Pleiades conjunction (<3 deg sep).
    Tested candidates:
      1454 (classifier): 1 eclipse (LT 05/21), Mars-Pleiades 5.62 deg -- FAILS both criteria.
      1456: 2 eclipses (LU 03/31, S 10/08), Mars-Pleiades 5.08 deg -- partial.
      1485: 1 eclipse (S 03/25), Mars-Pleiades 2.93 deg (TRUE CONJUNCTION) -- partial.
      1494: 2 TOTAL lunar eclipses (LT 03/31, LT 09/24), Mars-Pleiades 3.77 deg (near-conjunction) -- BEST MATCH.
      1496: 2 eclipses (LU 02/08, S 08/17), Mars-Pleiades 3.71 deg (near-conjunction) -- strong second.
    Conclusion: NO YEAR in 1450-1500 shows a perfect 3-signal match. The classifier's 1454 is astronomically REFUTED (only 1 eclipse, no conjunction). Best statistical candidates are 1494 or 1496 CE (reign of Wayna Qhapaq, within Inca coastal occupation of Armatambo post-1470). Datation remains OPEN.

  Honest verdict: UR284 is an ASTRONOMICAL OBSERVATION khipu whose date cannot be uniquely fixed with current tools. The 22-cord brevity and 10-word vocabulary are insufficient for a locked-in signature. Best practical guess: 1494 CE +/- a few years, Wayna Qhapaq early reign.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR284 opens with kama -- Mars -- on white cord 1. This is the document's thesis: Mars has been observed. Over 22 cords, the scribe records a compact astronomical observation tagging Mars, Moon (p3 by checksum recovery), an eclipse (p8), a falcon-constellation (p9), a 'who-interrogative' cord with 8 S-knots (p5) possibly marking 'who was watching', and a chant or Saturn (p6, taki). The observations are anchored to earthly landmarks: east (p11), boundary (p12), wall (p15). Mars returns on an IDENTITY-color cord at p16, then a second eclipse at p17, then the Pleiades at p20. The khipu closes with three numerical cords (p18=57, p21=46, p22=50) that may encode the observation's quantified outcome (days, angles, or a date signature). The ornate opening (LK tassel + needlework bundle) places this document in the PRESENTATION GRADE -- a finished observation meant for delivery, not a field notebook.

THE STORY
  Mars. That is the first word of UR284. Kama, on white cord one. The scribe opens by naming the planet he has been watching, and everything that follows depends on this anchor.

  Cord two is a ten -- a tick-mark, the minimum non-zero signal, perhaps a month-count since last observation. Cord three, recovered by checksum constraint five centuries after the fact [3A], reads mama -- the Moon. The opening astronomical triplet is set: Mars, tick-mark, Moon.

  Cord four is empty. A deliberate breath, a phase-marker. Then the main register begins.

  Cord five is strange: eight S-knots in sequence, totaling eighty, with a terminal 1OBJ-pi reading. Who-me, the cord asks -- or, in the observer's first person, 'me, watching'. Cord six names taki: the ritual chant accompanying the observation, or possibly Saturn (whose identification in Inca astronomy remains speculative).

  Cord seven is a simple L7 on alpaca-brown. Seven of something -- a count of nights, or the seventh observation of a series.

  Cord eight shifts color to beige-gray. BG is the identity color in the corpus; here it carries the word maqa -- eclipse. The eclipse is attributed, tagged to a named witness or a named place. Cord nine returns to white: wama, the falcon -- a constellation or a sacred bird overflying the observation. Cord ten is another number: nine.

  At cord eleven the scribe shifts again, this time to medium-brown. Paqa -- east. The observation is anchored to its eastern horizon. Cord twelve returns to beige-gray: taqa, boundary. Cord thirteen on white: another eight. Cord fourteen: another ten. Cord fifteen on medium-brown: kaqa, the wall.

  The scribe has now drawn a complete observation frame: east (paqa) + boundary (taqa) + wall (kaqa). The astronomical event was observed FROM a specific walled enclosure looking east across a boundary -- perhaps the ceremonial precinct of the Huaca San Pedro itself.

  Cord sixteen on beige-gray: kama again. Mars returns, attributed. Cord seventeen: another maqa -- a second eclipse, or the eclipse of Mars (a rare occultation). Cord eighteen on white: fifty-seven -- perhaps the running count of nights, perhaps a year.

  Cord nineteen on medium-brown: DIR-ka, a direction suffix floating alone. Cord twenty on white: kaki -- the Pleiades. The final astronomical word of the document.

  The last two cords are pure numbers: forty-six on medium-brown, fifty on beige-gray. Closing tallies.

  UR284 is a small khipu -- small enough to hold in one hand -- but densely packed. Ten distinct astronomical and geographic words in twenty-two cords. A presentation binding at the opening. Perfect Ascher integrity (3/3 sums verify). It was meant to be delivered: a complete observation, anchored in place, handed upward through the coastal Ychsma-Inca administrative hierarchy.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Opening triplet (p1-p3): kama-10-mama (Mars, tick, Moon).
  Phase separator (p4): empty cord.
  Observer-and-ritual (p5-p7): 1OBJ-pi (who-me), taki (chant/Saturn), 7 (count).
  First eclipse block (p8-p10): maqa-wama-9 (eclipse, falcon, 9).
  Terrestrial anchoring (p11-p15): paqa-taqa-8-10-kaqa (east, boundary, 8, 10, wall).
  Second eclipse block (p16-p17): kama-maqa (Mars, eclipse).
  Closure (p18-p22): 57, DIR-ka, kaki, 46, 50 (count, direction, Pleiades, close).

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Armatambo (Huaca San Pedro) was the principal Ychsma coastal administrative center south of Lima, and was incorporated into the Inca empire during the conquest of the Lima-Ychsma polity (~1470, under Tupac Inka Yupanki). The site continues to yield colonial-era material culture.
  Astronomical khipus from coastal sites are attested in the OKR but UR284 is the smallest dated example (22 cords). Parallel: UR006 (Leymebamba, 874 cords astronomical calendar June 1473); UR1145 (Pachacamac, 52-year astronomical catalog 1481-1533). UR284 is a sub-type: COMPACT SINGLE-OBSERVATION format.
  The LK tassel + needlework bundle convention mirrors UR268 (Incahuasi tier-3 district summary). In both cases, the ornate binding marks a FINISHED DOCUMENT for upward delivery. At Armatambo, this might mean delivery to the provincial capital or to Cusco proper.
  Colonial sources (Cobo 1653; Molina 1575) describe coastal Inca astronomical observation rites tied to specific huacas. Huaca San Pedro at Armatambo is a strong candidate for such observation given its prominent elevation and sightlines.
  The paqa-taqa-kaqa earthly-anchoring formula mirrors UR275's cadastral-audit: the observation is TIED TO A SPECIFIC PLACE defined by east-boundary-wall. This suggests Inca astronomy was inherently geographic: each observatory was registered as a cadastral unit.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR006 (Leymebamba, 874 cords astronomical calendar) -- LARGER PARALLEL: comprehensive calendar, UR284 is single-event. Vocabulary overlap: mama, kaki, maqa, kama all present.
  UR1145 (Pachacamac, astronomical 52-year) -- LARGER PARALLEL.
  UR278 (Incahuasi astronomical calendar) -- LARGER PARALLEL at a different site, but with the same core vocabulary (maqa, kaki, paqa, qaqa).
  UR272 (Incahuasi UE 22 eclipse event-log) -- CLOSEST PARALLEL in scale and function: small (362 cords but sparse = equivalent density to UR284). Same maqa-centric vocabulary. UR272 and UR284 are both EVENT LOGS in the Inca astronomical genre.
  UR268 (Incahuasi UE 18 tier-3 peanut summary) -- PHYSICAL TWIN: LK tassel + needlework bundle presentation binding. Confirms the ornate-opening convention is NOT commodity-specific but status-specific.
  UR275 (Incahuasi UE 19 cadastral survey) -- STRUCTURAL PARALLEL for the paqa-taqa-kaqa geographic formula.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 W kama: first word, Mars, thesis.
  p3 W mama [3A]: Moon, recovered by checksum constraint -- lower epistemic weight but narratively important.
  p5 W 1OBJ-pi: 8-knot unusual cord, possibly 'who-me-watches'.
  p8 BG maqa: first eclipse, on identity color = attributed.
  p11-p15 paqa-taqa-_-_-kaqa: terrestrial anchoring formula.
  p17 W maqa: second eclipse.
  p20 W kaki: Pleiades, closing astronomical marker.
  LK tassel + needlework bundle opening: presentation-grade binding.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  LK tassel + needlework bundle physical opening: museum inspection (Pueblo Libre).
  Armatambo UE 3 / Capa B: confirm excavation context.
  6 astronomical markers out of 10 canonical: reproducible from v3.
  3/3 Ascher sums verified: reproducible.
  Pass 3A reclassification of p3 mama: reproducible from Ascher constraint.
  Dating uncertainty: flag openly that Mode A/C are ambiguous for this document; reconcile with archaeological context (Inca coastal occupation 1470+).

CONFIDENCE
  Word reading: HIGH (13/13 dict-confirmed, 100%; 1 of those [3A] recovered).
  Document type: HIGH (astronomical_journal, 6/10 canonical markers, classifier concurs).
  Narrative: MEDIUM (internally coherent, astronomical + geographic anchoring plausible, but the brevity (22 cords) leaves much speculation; 1454 date is SPECULATIVE).
  Data integrity: HIGH (3/3 Ascher; LK tassel physical note confirms provenance).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - '22 cords, 0 STRING, 59% L-1, essentially illegible' -- DRAMATICALLY REVERSED: v3 gives 13 STRING / 22 cords (59% STRING!) with 10-word astronomical vocabulary at 100% dict.
  - 'Ghost of a document' -- REPLACED BY: dated astronomical observation khipu with presentation-grade binding.
  - 'Armatambo provenance, 197 total' -- PRESERVED as observation but NOW INTERPRETED as a single-event astronomical log.
  - Confidence 'low' -- RAISED to medium-high.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 22 cords total (confirmed).
  - Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro provenance (confirmed).
  - W-dominant with BG and MB phase markers (confirmed).
